messages.author: tmacinc
messages.text: I've been working on a backcountry team app for android that uses MeshCore infrastructure for keeping track of group members. It was created with hunting and backcountry recreation in mind, but I'm sure it would be useful for other things too. It has messaging via public channels, private channels and direct messaging, and a map system that allows for offline use, tracking of group members and waypoint sharing. It is also intended to color code users on the map based on the hops for real time SA of connectivity. I've hosted the apk in the repo here. https://github.com/tmacinc/meshcore-team-alpha Keep in mind that it's alpha, but I'm looking for feedback on bugs or potential feature that people would like to see. I'm also debating making it open source if there's some interest in helping out. One thing I'm likely unable to do alone is create version for iOS. Team is the working title and I'm open to input.

messages.author: focaldistorsion
messages.text: Thank you for the time to build this app. Feedback as of release 0.4.3 name: Mesh TEAM is suited as it focuses solely on team work. Name should be the same in App list in Android to keep all things related Mesh closely night. Focus: not only hunting, back country but also day to day tracking. Tactical Emergency Area Messaging is useful in plenty of situations, especially crisis. a short splash screen with logo and name on first launch could improve the transition from os to app (ability to customize/disable it in settings) Connection tab scan button icon is a retry icon, not intuitive on first scan that's where you are have to tap on to connect. change icon to something related to connecting . Add icon in the description text. if device not found update to retry icon. pull and display Radio settings from companion Location Tracking Settings: add option to change location update time interval in minutes. Map tab Ring interval: make it customizable in radius aside predefined options App settings: customizable accent color light and dark mode I one would use this over other tested apps as a background app on family phones for passive tracking with T1000s over Google location sharing which is at often crap at updating. Meshcore SAR is a good candidate but too tactical for a high WAF (Wife Acceptance Factor). Concept can be expanded with EXFIL plans which can be shared created in the phone to other phones via BT and have the waypoints displayed from the list. Current waypoint creation would help in adjusting the premade plans/waypoints on the go. Take a look at the ReadyPlan app for more on this topic. (edited)

messages.text: Future ideas for concept development Refactoring Bottom Nav Bar UX 1. Contacts (add) separated from messages for visiblity and ease of use add filters (companion, repeater, room server), default selected companion as the purpose of the app is to message other companions 2. Team (add) create team(s) link contacts from existing Contacts link to private existing Channel move Location Tracking Settings and adjust to work on a channel basis: e.g. Hiking team tracking is event based, Family team is constant. toggle switch to disable team tracking. 3. Messages it is logical to have channels and DMs under messages dual tab to switch between the two 4. Map 5. Settings (edited)
messages.content_html:
Future ideas for concept development Refactoring Bottom Nav Bar UX 1. Contacts (add)
  • separated from messages for visiblity and ease of use
  • add filters (companion, repeater, room server), default selected companion as the purpose of the app is to message other companions
2. Team (add)
  • create team(s)
    • link contacts from existing Contacts
    • link to private existing Channel
    • move Location Tracking Settings and adjust to work on a channel basis: e.g. Hiking team tracking is event based, Family team is constant. toggle switch to disable team tracking.
3. Messages
  • it is logical to have channels and DMs under messages
  • dual tab to switch between the two
4. Map 5. Settings (edited)

messages.text: Major update again https://github.com/tmacinc/meshcore-team-alpha/releases/tag/v0.8.0-beta . This one tackles backcountry meshing and enables forwarding on companions using custom firmware. Forwarding attempts to use a smart system based on current mesh status with your group to minimize repeats and network usage. Defaults to off and reverts when it's not needed. Firmware link is in the release notes, also here https://github.com/tmacinc/MeshCore . You'll have to build it yourself if you want to try it

messages.author: tmacinc
messages.text: All, just an update on this. My backcountry testing didnt go so well, not because of the app, algorithm or meshcore. Every single one of my friends I was with is an Apple user. So I didnt get to do anything beyond 2 users with my 2 phones. I've hit a pretty big cross roads with this because it's really only usable if it works on both phone types. I have a pretty major update sitting in my dev branch that I havent released, I've implemented the forwarding algorithm which required a restructuring of the telemetry packets. I took the time to reduce the bytes and optimize it as best I can. Theres a new firmware version that handles the smart forwarding and it all needs more testing with more devices to ensure it doesn't completely break down. With the lack of iOS support I've really lost some drive and have been weighing my options going forward. Ive considered forking MeshCore-Open and rebuilding the backcountry functionality of team as a feature, with the hopes that it would be included in the official version, and Ive reached out to the MeshCore dev to see if the firmware changes might align or enhance the future camp mode. I really think all of this would be awesome to be part of the official releases so its available to all users when they need it without needing to flash firmware and setup specific apps before trips. The only other real option I have is for an iOS dev to take on the other version as it's outside of what I can do. Both of the discussions with MeshCore and MeshCore-Open were positive but brief, here are the links if anyone would like to comment. Ill probably decide a way forward in the next week. https://github.com/meshcore-dev/MeshCore/discussions/1650 https://github.com/zjs81/meshcore-open/issues/127

messages.author: tmacinc
messages.text: Another update on Team. Im in the process of rebuilding it as a Flutter app. I went back and forth on building on MeshCore-Open vs starting from scratch and I feel that MeshCore-Open already has a lot of complexity and settings that would make it hard to keep the set up simple, which is a primary goal of mine. I also ran into a few pretty major conflicts in core data storage that would make integration a challenge. I've currently got the db, basic ui, messaging, contacts and BLE layers working. Once I get the map system and telemetry functioning you can expect an apk for comments compared to the previous version and for any major bugs. Once Im happy that it functions relatively closely to the original, the plan is to make the project open source so we can get the other versions working. It also looks like bringing up the discussion of getting forwarding in the firmware natively got them to prioritize and release it. Ill be integrating the functionality as an option if people dont have the Team firmware, but for full functionality, custom firmware will still be required. At least now both cases can use companion repeats which is great. Ill have to think about how to track and integrate radio settings with mixed usage, but ill work on that once the app is usable again.

messages.text: I just submitted version 0.10.0-beta for review/release through the closed testing link. Lot's of updates in this one. - Fixed companion GPS and added check for has_valid_fix handling. PR currently open with stock firmware. Added V1 forwarding algorithm so the app is now at parity with the old android only version. Added autonomous tracker mode. This allows a companion to be configured in the app, then disconnected and set free and it will send telemetry to the TEAM on it's own. The new functionality requires the TEAM version of the firmware found here. https://github.com/tmacinc/MeshCore The app handles firmware version checks and handling options depending on which firmware is in use. Also added a capability check during network discovery that advertises what firmware/options are enabled on each team member. If we could get some more testers it would be great!

messages.author: tmacinc
messages.text: You are able to enable camp mode on either firmware version, this is the one that forces you to a frequency to use. I've done it with a preset system so when you enable it, the settings require a preset choice and then it will enable it on save. If your using the custom firmware you can also enable smart forwarding in this mode. Smart forwarding runs by default when not in camp mode. the V1 version that is live works based on the members of your team that are sharing telemetry. So if you are all zero hop, it disables forwarding. It then expands network wide to be 1 hop beyond what the max observed hops are for your group. It's intended to reduce traffic so you can utilize more telemetry or just get less collisions. V2 is a lot more complicated and I'll be adding it next. The smart forwarding only works during tracking. If you're just using the app without tracking it runs as normal with camp mode using forwarding and none camp mode not using forwarding. (edited)

messages.text: Release Notes — v1.0.3 Routes: Users can now create, edit and share routes via the mesh to their group. Route Colors Routes can now be assigned a color from a 10-color preset palette when saving. Route colors are shared over the mesh network and displayed on the map. Color is preserved when editing routes and when receiving multi-part routes. ## Contact Path History Contact movement trails can be displayed on the map. Paths render as dotted lines (black with white outline) with dots at each GPS fix. Global "Show Contact Paths" toggle in the map settings menu to show/hide all paths. Per-contact "Show Path" / "Hide Path" button in the contact details dialog. Global and per-contact toggles stay in sync — toggling all on/off updates individual states, and individually hiding the last contact turns off the global toggle. 25-meter stationary gate prevents point clustering when a contact is not moving. Position history is thinned to 50 real GPS points per contact (no averaging). ## GPX Export GPX export now uses a file picker dialog on both Android and iOS instead of saving to a hardcoded Downloads directory. ## Bug Fixes Fixed keyboard overflow when saving a route with a long name or description. Fixed received multi-part routes losing their color. Fixed literal \n showing in the manage waypoints subtitle instead of a newline. Removed "Route" from the waypoint type dropdown in the create waypoint dialog. (edited)
messages.content_html:
Release Notes — v1.0.3

Routes: Users can now create, edit and share routes via the mesh to their group.

Route Colors

  • Routes can now be assigned a color from a 10-color preset palette when saving.
  • Route colors are shared over the mesh network and displayed on the map.
  • Color is preserved when editing routes and when receiving multi-part routes. ## Contact Path History
  • Contact movement trails can be displayed on the map.
  • Paths render as dotted lines (black with white outline) with dots at each GPS fix.
  • Global "Show Contact Paths" toggle in the map settings menu to show/hide all paths.
  • Per-contact "Show Path" / "Hide Path" button in the contact details dialog.
  • Global and per-contact toggles stay in sync — toggling all on/off updates individual states, and individually hiding the last contact turns off the global toggle.
  • 25-meter stationary gate prevents point clustering when a contact is not moving.
  • Position history is thinned to 50 real GPS points per contact (no averaging). ## GPX Export
  • GPX export now uses a file picker dialog on both Android and iOS instead of saving to a hardcoded Downloads directory. ## Bug Fixes
  • Fixed keyboard overflow when saving a route with a long name or description.
  • Fixed received multi-part routes losing their color.
  • Fixed literal \n showing in the manage waypoints subtitle instead of a newline.
  • Removed "Route" from the waypoint type dropdown in the create waypoint dialog.
 (edited)

messages.author: tmacinc
messages.text: Release Notes — v1.0.3-beta2 Team Config Export / Import New Create Team Config , Import Team Config , and Share Config Offline options in the Connection screen menu (visible when connected). Export channels, waypoints, radio settings, and offline map tiles as a portable .teamcfg.zip file. Named configs — give each export a descriptive name (stored in the manifest). Per-item selection — choose exactly which channels, waypoints, and map areas to include. Radio settings export includes frequency, bandwidth, spreading factor, and coding rate (TX power excluded — each radio keeps its own value). Import requires an active companion connection — channels are registered with the firmware, radio settings applied, waypoints merged (dedup by meshId + name/location), and map tiles added to cache. Two import methods: From File (local file picker) or From QR Code (scan and download from a nearby device). Preview dialog shows full config contents before importing. Offline map tiles are packaged inside the ZIP and restored into the tile cache on import. File saved via system file picker; compatible with Android and iOS. ## Offline Config Sharing New Share Config Offline option — serve a .teamcfg.zip over a local Wi-Fi hotspot without internet. Guided setup: platform-specific hotspot instructions (Android/iOS), file picker, config confirmation, then one-tap serving. Displays a QR code for receivers to scan from the Import Team Config → From QR Code flow. Manual URL fallback shown below the QR code. Download counter tracks how many devices have fetched the config. Download progress bar with MB counter when importing via QR code. ## Wipe Local Data New Wipe Local Data option in the Connection screen menu. Choose which data to clear: private channels, waypoints & routes, offline maps. Channels are cleared from the companion radio firmware before being removed from the local database.
messages.content_html:
Release Notes — v1.0.3-beta2

Team Config Export / Import

  • New Create Team Config, Import Team Config, and Share Config Offline options in the Connection screen menu (visible when connected).
  • Export channels, waypoints, radio settings, and offline map tiles as a portable .teamcfg.zip file.
  • Named configs — give each export a descriptive name (stored in the manifest).
  • Per-item selection — choose exactly which channels, waypoints, and map areas to include.
  • Radio settings export includes frequency, bandwidth, spreading factor, and coding rate (TX power excluded — each radio keeps its own value).
  • Import requires an active companion connection — channels are registered with the firmware, radio settings applied, waypoints merged (dedup by meshId + name/location), and map tiles added to cache.
  • Two import methods: From File (local file picker) or From QR Code (scan and download from a nearby device).
  • Preview dialog shows full config contents before importing.
  • Offline map tiles are packaged inside the ZIP and restored into the tile cache on import.
  • File saved via system file picker; compatible with Android and iOS. ## Offline Config Sharing
  • New Share Config Offline option — serve a .teamcfg.zip over a local Wi-Fi hotspot without internet.
  • Guided setup: platform-specific hotspot instructions (Android/iOS), file picker, config confirmation, then one-tap serving.
  • Displays a QR code for receivers to scan from the Import Team Config → From QR Code flow.
  • Manual URL fallback shown below the QR code.
  • Download counter tracks how many devices have fetched the config.
  • Download progress bar with MB counter when importing via QR code. ## Wipe Local Data
  • New Wipe Local Data option in the Connection screen menu.
  • Choose which data to clear: private channels, waypoints & routes, offline maps.
  • Channels are cleared from the companion radio firmware before being removed from the local database.

messages.text: MeshCore-TEAM firmware has been updated to 1.15.0.4. I think I've figured out how to auto-build the firmware assets so they should be available under the Releases section in the next few hours. Note that since MeshCore has been adding constants in the last 2 updates, I've shifted the team specific constants to avoid conflicts going forward. This means that you will need to update both the app and the firmware for all functionality to be working if you are using TEAM firmware functions. The app has been updated in the Play store to Version 1.1.0. This includes the path history tracking, and the configuration creation, sharing and importing tools that have been in beta testing for the last few weeks. Both the public and beta versions are currently updated to 1.1.0. I'm not planning additional updates until the iOS approval goes through, at which point I will update that side also. If there are any bugs please let me know. https://github.com/tmacinc/MeshCore
